findings.summary: The archived hidden-prerequisite map is stale enough to mis-sequence current follow-on work because multiple formerly missing contracts now exist in shipped packets and runtime code.
findings.recommended_fix: Refresh the master roadmap into implemented/narrowed/reopened/still-open states before using the archived ordering as the default current-state plan.

findings.summary: Operational acceptance for indexing and scan correctness is still open even after packet 010 and packet 003/003 landed their design-level fixes.
findings.recommended_fix: Restart MCP in a network-enabled runtime, rerun the live scan on 026/009, and close the 003/003 recovery packet before treating scan/index results as authoritative.

findings.summary: Search-routing efficacy is still measured mostly by predictive telemetry; the best current static result is 112/200 top-1 matches and the live wrapper remains observe-only.
findings.recommended_fix: Finish the blocked Codex config path and collect live wrapper telemetry before upgrading routing-efficacy claims.

findings.summary: Cross-runtime hook parity is incomplete because Copilot wrapper schema and writer wiring were reverted even though Codex parity shipped and passed live smoke tests.
findings.recommended_fix: Reapply packets 010 and 011, then rerun the cross-runtime smoke matrix.

findings.summary: Trust-axis separation is now partly implemented in shared payload and readiness code, but the master narrative still treats it as if it were only a future prerequisite.
findings.recommended_fix: Update the root packet to distinguish shipped trust contracts from remaining consumer-coverage and live-acceptance gaps.

findings.summary: The archived continuity gap around a missing Stop-summary artifact is stale; the current question is artifact shape and consumer usage under the planner-first contract.
findings.recommended_fix: Rename the root continuity gap and document the planner-first plus compact-wrapper contract explicitly.

findings.summary: Routing now spans at least two planes — front-door subsystem dispatch and inner memory-search channel routing — and prior iterations never reconciled them in one operator-facing explanation.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a root-level routing architecture note and tests that make the two planes explicit.

findings.summary: The archived convergence label conflated research coverage with operational completion, which aged poorly once later packets introduced reverts, warnings, and live-acceptance tails.
findings.recommended_fix: Use separate synthesis and operational convergence states in future dashboards and registries.
